hero_episodes.title: AI Rebuilt Every YC W26 Startup. Should Founders Be Scared?
hero_episodes.headline: Boardy, $17M traction, and Jason's shift from AI vibes to tabular rigor

trajectory_delta.intervention_note: Jason's filter shifts from AI vibes to measurable tabular-data proof.

trajectory_delta.then_label: Publicly framing inference cost as the core enterprise AI bottleneck
trajectory_delta.now_label: Project Glasswing context, inference-time compute moat
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: The edge shifts from training spectacle to inference-time systems advantage.

trajectory_delta.now_label: Agent economics framed around roughly $2,000/month AI subscription burden
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: The business lives or dies on whether workflow value exceeds model spend.

trajectory_delta.then_label: AI video founder in category-building phase
trajectory_delta.now_label: $4B valuation, $200M Series E led by GV
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: Applied AI category winners compound by owning workflow outcomes, not general intelligence.

the_misses.at_the_time: Regulatory risk and belief that black-car demand was niche
the_misses.logic_then: Passed on regulatory and market-structure risk because the category looked capped by legacy transport rules.
the_misses.reality_now: Liquidity, software trust, and demand aggregation turned a constrained service into a platform-scale winner.
the_misses.lesson: When software rewrites market structure, old category ceilings break fast.

the_misses.at_the_time: Trust, safety, and social discomfort looked fatal
the_misses.logic_then: Passed on trust risk in 2009 because mainstream behavior looked too brittle for scale.
the_misses.reality_now: Software trust layers, ratings, identity, and repeat reputation loops proved to be the $78.04B unlock by 2026.
the_misses.lesson: When software absorbs trust risk, a category that looked impossible can become inevitable.

masterclass_featured.title: Anthropic’s Mythos, Project Glasswing, and the inference-time compute moat

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Barksdale Filter
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: Move from AI vibes to tabular-data rigor. Strong opinions only matter when backed by measured outcomes.

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Subscription Reality
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: If the agent stack costs $2,000/month, it must eliminate more than $2,000/month of real work.

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Inference-Time Moat
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: In applied AI, inference-time compute and routing may be more defensible than raw model training.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: E2271, rigor over vibes
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: Jason frames the next phase of AI company analysis around tabular evidence, not charismatic demos.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: E2272, the $2,000/month test
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: Alex Finn's pricing thesis turns agent adoption into a hard operating-economics conversation.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: E2273, Project Glasswing
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: The real debate is not just model power. It is whether inference-time systems become the durable moat.
